sprint.title: Data Retention v1: 90-Day Policy + Account Delete + Scheduled Purge (Convex + R2)
sprint.goal: Implement a user-safe, compliant data lifecycle: 90-day retention, account delete with undo window, and scheduled purge that removes personal data and media from storage. Provide clear UX and policy documentation.
definitionOfDone: Data retention policy is implemented: purge personal data after 90 days post-deletion (or last activity if you choose)
Account delete flow exists (soft delete immediately, purge scheduled)
Undo deletion is available until purge time
Purge job deletes Convex records and R2 media assets
User can export/download their data before deletion (optional but recommended)
Privacy pages updated with retention + deletion policy
All actions logged for support (no sensitive content in logs)

stackAssumptions.privacy: UK GDPR storage limitation + right to erasure concepts

sourceNotes.retention: UK GDPR requires retention to be justified and not longer than necessary; right to erasure exists.

backlog.title: Define retention policy & events (90-day window) + document it in product/legal

policy: Account deletion triggers a 90-day retention window (undo available)
After 90 days: purge/anonymize personal data + delete media
If user requests erasure: handle per policy unless legal basis requires retention
docs: Privacy policy section: retention and deletion

acceptanceTests: Policy documented clearly and matches actual behavior

backlog.title: Schema changes: deletion status + scheduled purge timestamps (users + storybooks + media)

constraints: Soft delete should immediately block login/use (or block access to content) depending on your product decision

acceptanceTests: Deletion fields persist and are queryable
Soft-deleted users are treated correctly by route guards

backlog.title: Account delete UX (confirm, reason optional, export data reminder) + undo flow

behavior: Undo clears deletion status and restores access

acceptanceTests: Delete marks account pending deletion and user sees pending state
Undo restores account and content access

backlog.title: Purge job orchestration (Convex scheduled job) + R2 media deletion

job: Daily scheduled job scans for users where purgeAt <= now
Deletes/anonymizes: user profile, storybooks, answers, studio docs, billing links (keep minimal non-PII ledger if needed)
Deletes R2 objects for user uploads (story photos, cached uploads)
constraints: Idempotent: running twice does not break

acceptanceTests: Purged user data is no longer retrievable
R2 assets are deleted (or marked and later deleted) reliably

backlog.title: Access control changes: block pending-deletion users and hide content from UI

behavior: If user.pending_deletion: block Studio/Book routes and show pending deletion page with Undo

acceptanceTests: Pending deletion users cannot access content pages

backlog.title: Optional: Data export before deletion (download JSON/PDF links)

export: Provide 'Download your data' on delete confirm
Export includes storybooks metadata + answers + list of uploads (not necessarily the media bytes in v1)

acceptanceTests: User can download their data package before deletion

backlog.title: QA + tests (delete, undo, purge scheduling, access blocking)

automation: Integration: delete sets pending + purgeAt, undo clears
Integration: purge job removes records and marks user deleted

acceptanceTests: At least 5 tests added and passing
No accidental deletion of active users

notes: UK GDPR storage limitation: you decide retention but must justify it and avoid keeping data longer than necessary.
Right to erasure exists and should be supported via account deletion + purge.
